segments.text: Jack: Great Countryside Holidays, Jack speaking.
Jack: Can I help you?
Woman: Yes, I hope so.
Woman: I've heard that your travel agency provides holidays in the countryside.
Jack: That's right.
Jack: We've specialized in countryside holidays with many years of experience, so we can ensure that we provide perfect holidays with several destinations.
Woman: That's great!
Woman: My husband and I are planning a countryside holiday with the whole family, and I'm calling just for the accommodation.
Jack: OK.
Jack: Where are you going to spend your holiday?
Jack: There're some excellent accommodations on our list in different destinations.
Woman: Well, last year we spent our holiday in the southern part of the city, but this time we'd like to live in the northeast.
Woman: I have a friend who's just returned and had great fun there.
Jack: OK.
Jack: And what about the time of your holiday?
Woman: It'll be in the first week of September, which we think will be off-peak travel time.
Woman: At peak season, the attractions are always crowded with people, and hotels or restaurants might overcharge you, so we try to avoid that.
Jack: Yes, arranging your holiday during off-peak time is wise as you can enjoy the attractions and facilities better.
Woman: That's right.
Jack: Then how long are you going to stay?
Woman: Our holiday will last three days, and we will stay there for a weekend.
Jack: OK.
Jack: That's the location and time of the holiday.
Jack: Then let's turn to the accommodation itself.
Jack: Do you have any particular requirements for housing?
Woman: Yes, the first priority we've considered is, of course, a quiet environment so that both young and old can have good rest during our stay.
Jack: No problem.
Woman: Then we'd prefer a cottage on a good beach where my children can play with great fun.
Woman: My little daughter loves collecting shells and building sandcastles.
Jack: OK.
Jack: I'll take that down.
Jack: A seaside cottage is excellent.
Jack: It also means secluded nights and fresh sea air.
Woman: Right.
Woman: Oh, another thing is, I'm kind of a foodie, and whenever I visit a new place, I'm always desperate to try the local cuisine.
Woman: So if possible, I'd prefer a good restaurant not far away from where we live.
Jack: No problem.
Jack: Tasting the local cuisine is one of the delights of the holiday.
Jack: Then what about the actual room?
Jack: Are there any other requirements?
Woman: Our family has five members, so we'll need a cottage with three living rooms and a separate bathroom with a shower.
Jack: OK.
Jack: Three living rooms and a separate bathroom with a shower.
Woman: Oh, another important thing is, my father got his backache last week, and climbing stairs might cause trouble.
Woman: I'm concerned that he might not recover from it during our holiday, so there shouldn't be any stairs in the cottage, I'm afraid.
Jack: No stairs.
Jack: OK, I've taken that down.
Jack: Do you need a balcony?
Woman: Actually, it's not necessary for us, but if possible, we need a garden, you know, where the children can play.
Jack: No problem.
Jack: Several cottages cater to your need.
Woman: Great!
Woman: Well, that's all our needs for the accommodation.
Jack: OK, I've written down all the details.
Jack: And I'll contact you as soon as I find the one which can satisfy your needs.
Woman: Thank you.
Jack: I'll need your personal details.
Jack: What's your surname?
Woman: Cheffins, that's C-H-E-double F-I-N-S.
Jack: OK, Mrs Cheffins.
Woman: And my telephone number is 0192477285.
Jack: All right.
Jack: Before we finish, I'd like to know where you heard about us.
Jack: Did your friend tell you?
Woman: Actually, not.
Woman: I saw the ads of your travel agency in a magazine called Countryside Living.
Jack: I see.
Jack: That's a popular magazine.
Woman: Yes.
Woman: Thank you very much.
Jack: My pleasure, Mrs Cheffins.
Jack: Bye!
